title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 17th December 2025 | TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Hold; technical rally toward 800 possible, maybe before March
recommendations.detail: Says hold despite the ~50% drawdown. Lots of churning and some company changes, but on the electrical side a revival is likely at some point. Chart is weaker but less weak than peers; expects a technical rally toward ~800, possibly before March. Warns it is a small (<Rs 1,000 cr) demerged micro-cap whose moves can be wild.

recommendations.note: Hold now; exit into any rally after Q3 results
recommendations.detail: A hype-driven name of the last two years now punished by the market; once ~Rs 4,000 cr m-cap at listing, since halved and trading near its 52-week low. Last-quarter results were weakish but the order book is said to be improving, so watch the Q3 result and exit into any rally. Not cheap at 46 PE for the segment.

recommendations.note: Don't sell; hold, now coming to a buying price
recommendations.detail: Pharma is churning across names; the stock corrected slow and steady post ex-rights (from ~250-260) as cheap-rights holders and institutions sold. But last-quarter results beat expectations and the PE has come to ~30, so it is actually coming to a buying price. Advises not to sell and to hold.

recommendations.note: Don't average; trim position on rallies
recommendations.detail: Advises against averaging despite a 21% loss. Post-demerger sum-of-the-parts is good and profit-booking was advised earlier; now the business is moving into a weak wicket, with Ashok Leyland strong in CVs and Mahindra strong in PVs. Prices sustain only if new-model launches click and numbers improve. Watch carefully; trimming the position on rallies is advisable.

recommendations.note: Don't buy now (117 PE); good buy only near strong support at 260
recommendations.detail: As of now advises against buying. Not a small, cheap stock at 117 PE and now an institutional F&O counter. Flags a three-way quick-commerce fight (Blinkit vs Swiggy Instamart vs Zepto), with Swiggy having raised money via QIP. There is strong support around 260 where it becomes a good buy; immediately, no.

recommendations.note: Good bet at current valuation; 15-20% returns over next year
recommendations.detail: Not a cheap stock (~150 PE) but among property players it has a good land bank and very strong founders. Compared to reality stocks it can be a good bet at current valuation. Believes it can give decent 15-20% returns over the next one year.

recommendations.note: Good stock; hold for good returns over the years
recommendations.detail: Calls it a good stock with no need to be afraid. Advises holding; decent enough to give good returns over a multi-year holding period.
Now a pan-India-presence NBFC; recently, when it fell to losses, institutional investors bought in. Advises holding with no worry; decent enough to give good returns over the holding years.

Earlier flagged as a 'hero or zero' recent idea. Has his own doubts whether the best-segment returns are really there, though the large issue drew some institutional interest. Immediately advises holding; would not suggest fresh buying.

recommendations.note: Good but global-parent risk; doubts on outperformance
recommendations.detail: A good share but a globally-present company, so how the US parent's stock moves drags the Indian stock without warning. It has become cheaper and is near a 52-week low, but Oracle's US bonds sank sharply and there is a risk of drift toward junk status. Has his own doubts whether the Indian stock will outperform.

recommendations.note: Large-cap defence to bottom-fish; avoid mid/small-cap defence
recommendations.detail: For defence exposure, advises sticking to large caps, which have corrected to good valuations. Diversified large caps like Bharat Forge are likely to return to the limelight in coming days, so some bottom fishing there is reasonable. Searching in mid/small-cap defence stocks is not advisable as of now.

A decent auto-ancillary that gave good returns and outperformed peers, hitting a 52-week high just a couple of weeks back and sustaining even in weak days. Shows extremely good strength, so advises holding with a trailing stop near 1600.
Already up since he flagged it as an investment idea. The demerger opens a value-unlock possibility and it trades at only ~20 PE, so he would recommend Vedanta as of now. Expects some outperformance.
Asked to pick one among Hindustan Copper, Hindustan Zinc and Vedanta, he prefers Vedanta but says if you want to play the momentum game instead, then Hindustan Zinc. All three are in a good uptrend.
